steps.title: Use a Desmos regression shortcut
steps.content:
This is an equivalent-expression question, so a fast SAT-safe check is to make a table of values for the original expression and fit the result to a cubic. Use four $x$-values because the highest power is $x^3$:
$$x_1=[-2,-1,0,1]$$
$$y_1=(7x_1^3+7x_1)-(6x_1^3-3x_1)$$
$$y_1\sim ax_1^3+bx_1^2+cx_1+d$$

steps.title: Read the regression result
steps.content:
Desmos returns $a=1$, $b=0$, $c=10$, and $d=0$. That means the original expression behaves like:
$$x^3+10x$$
This already points to choice A.

steps.title: Confirm with algebra
steps.content:
Distribute the minus sign across the second parentheses:
$$(7x^3+7x)-(6x^3-3x)=7x^3+7x-6x^3+3x$$
Combine like terms:
$$(7x^3-6x^3)+(7x+3x)=x^3+10x$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is exactly $x^3+10x$. Choices B and C have the wrong cubic coefficient, and choice D has the wrong linear coefficient. Therefore A is correct.
